title: Adopt br as the project task tracker
description: Add tool-br skill and enforce beads issue id in commit messages via a standalone git hook.
close_reason: tool-br skill and beads-commit-msg hook added and committed in 0220a35.

title: Rewrite core AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/copilot-instructions.md fragments for genericity and signal
description: Researched external agent-instruction repos (lattice, gastownhall/beads, JuliusBrussee skills, mattpocock/skills, ponytail) and redesigned the .basicly/core/fragments catalog: moved repo-specific content (project-defaults, python-style) into .basicly-local overlay, rewrote weak fragments (core-rules, quality-gate, if-stuck->decision-protocol, no-user-path-leakage->secure-coding) with concrete, high-signal guidance, added new generic fragments (knowledge-priming, session-completion, non-interactive-shell). Also fixed a real bug: applyTo in generated copilot instructions must be a string not a YAML array. Removed redundant source-fragments comment from generated file headers (context poisoning with no tooling use).
close_reason: Fragment catalog redesigned and committed in 0220a35: core fragments genericized, weak content rewritten, applyTo bug fixed.

title: Support Conventional Commits breaking-change marker and add conventional-commits skill
description: commit-msg.py rejected the spec's '!' breaking-change indicator (feat!:, feat(scope)!:) with no way to express it. Added optional '!' support before the colon, shifted description group index, added regression tests. Also added .basicly/core/skills/conventional-commits/SKILL.md (with skill-creator-conformant Inputs/Outputs sections and trigger-quality validation) since no existing skill taught the full commit message format proactively before the hooks reject it.
close_reason: Breaking-change '!' support, conventional-commits skill, and clarified lowercase-description wording all committed (404adab, 25f4e7b).

title: test_cli.py mutates real repo generated-manifest.json instead of using isolated copies
description: run_basicly() ran build/skills-build/check against the real REPO_ROOT for 6 tests instead of an isolated tmp copy, rewriting .basicly/generated-manifest.json's timestamp on every test run and dirtying the working tree after every pytest invocation (including pre-push). Fixed by adding a work_repo tmp_path fixture and routing all CLI invocations through an isolated copy, consolidating the 3 tests that already did this manually via local copytree calls.
close_reason: Fixed via work_repo tmp_path fixture in tests/test_cli.py (727af06).

title: harden commit-msg description rules and add self-improvement retro fragment
description: Two fixes from this session's real mistakes: (1) commit-msg.py and the conventional-commits skill said description must 'start lowercase' but the actual rule requires the whole description to be lowercase with no underscores -- both real rejections I hit. Clarified wording + added concrete invalid examples in both places. (2) Added project/self-improvement-retro fragment: at session end, if a real rejection/mistake occurred, propose the exact fragment/skill/hook wording fix that would have prevented it, present for explicit approval, never self-apply. Also deduped two now-redundant bullets (core-rules secrets/validation line duplicated by the broadened secure-coding fragment) to stay under the copilot 6000-char budget.
close_reason: Committed in 18040c3.

title: fix ruff I001 import-sort violation masked by stale local .ruff_cache
description: GH Actions quality-gates failed ruff check on tests/test_loader.py and tests/test_skills.py (I001: missing blank line between third-party and first-party import groups). Every local ruff check this session reported clean because a stale .ruff_cache (gitignored, not present in CI's fresh checkout) was serving a false-clean cached result for these two files. Fixed the actual import formatting with ruff --fix after clearing the cache; verified clean with cache cleared.
close_reason: Fixed in de95c83.

title: fold beads close into the resolving commit instead of a trailing chore commit
description: tool-br skill said to close issues 'when finished' without specifying commit sequencing, so I was closing after committing the fix, producing a separate near-empty chore: close <id> commit every time (e.g. 08e5e6a had zero code value). Updated Safe Defaults/Common Pitfalls to close before the resolving commit and fold .beads/issues.jsonl into it; amend if forgotten and unpushed, accept a trailing commit only if already pushed.
close_reason: tool-br skill updated to close-before-commit sequencing; this very commit demonstrates it.

title: basicly v0.1 release: uvx-installable, consumer-ready
description: Umbrella epic for the initial distributable release of basicly (arch docs/architecture.md §9). Goal: a fresh consumer repo (e.g. terminal) can run 'uvx --from git+... basicly init' then build/skills-build/hooks-build to get working AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/copilot-instructions.md, skills, and git hooks. Success = both halves (guidance + gates) install cleanly from a pinned package. Children track the roadmap gaps in §11.

title: Move jinja2 from dev group to [project.dependencies]
description: §11.2: renderers/common.py imports jinja2 at runtime but jinja2 is only in [dependency-groups].dev. An installed wheel would crash at build time. Move jinja2 (and any other runtime import currently dev-only) into [project.dependencies]; verify nothing runtime-imported remains dev-only.
close_reason: Moved jinja2 from [dependency-groups].dev to [project.dependencies]; uv.lock now lists it as a runtime dep of basicly; import smoke test passes.

title: Enable packaging: add [build-system], flip tool.uv.package, keep console entry point
description: §11.1: add a [build-system] table (hatchling or equivalent), remove/flip tool.uv.package=false so the src-layout package builds. [project.scripts] basicly=basicly.cli:main already exists — confirm it resolves from a built wheel.
close_reason: Added [build-system] (hatchling) + [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] packages=['src/basicly'] and flipped tool.uv.package=true. 'uv build' produces wheel+sdist; 'uv run basicly --help' and 'uvx --from dist/*.whl basicly --help' both resolve basicly.cli:main. 55 tests pass.

title: Bundle the core catalog as package data so init can materialize it
description: §4.1/§11.6: .basicly/core (fragments, skills, hooks, targets, templates) lives outside src/ and is NOT in the wheel by default. A fresh consumer install has no catalog. Ship the catalog inside the package (e.g. src/basicly/catalog/ or force-include) so 'basicly init' can copy it onto a consumer's disk. Keep this repo's dogfooded .basicly/core as the authoring source; define the source->packaged sync story.
close_reason: Catalog now ships in the wheel: hatchling force-include maps .basicly/core -> basicly/catalog (53 files: 12 fragments, 25 skills, 5 hooks, 3 targets, 8 templates), pyc excluded; sdist carries .basicly/core so git+ installs work. Added basicly.catalog.bundled_catalog_root() locator (packaged copy, falls back to .basicly/core for source/dogfood) + tests. 57 tests pass.

title: Verify uvx install end-to-end from a clean environment
description: §9 quality gate: from a scratch dir, run 'uvx --from git+<repo>@<ref> basicly --help' and 'basicly list/build' against a materialized catalog; confirm the entry point resolves, jinja2 is present, and the catalog is found. Documents the real consumer install command.
close_reason: Verified end-to-end from a built wheel: 'uvx --from dist/*.whl basicly --version' -> 0.1.0; entry point resolves basicly.cli; catalog locatable; in a clean consumer dir the installed basicly materialized the catalog and ran 'list' + 'build' producing AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/copilot-instructions.md. jinja2 present as runtime dep. Documented the working install in architecture §9/§11. Caveat: the live git+<remote>@<ref> path is validated structurally (sdist ships .basicly/core; wheel-from-sdist build succeeds) but not run against a remote ref since these commits are unpushed; full one-command flow lands with 'init' (basicly-xwt).

title: Implement 'basicly init' (basicly.toml + overlay skeleton + materialize core catalog)
description: §8/§11.8: scaffold basicly.toml [paths], create .basicly-local/fragments/user/, and materialize .basicly/core from the bundled package catalog (depends on catalog bundling). Idempotent, never overwrites existing overlay content. Print next-steps (build / skills-build / hooks-build).
close_reason: Implemented 'basicly init': materializes the bundled core catalog into .basicly/core (never overwriting), creates the .basicly-local/fragments/user overlay skeleton, scaffolds basicly.toml if absent, and prints next steps. Idempotent and edit-preserving; guards against copying onto itself in the dogfood repo (src==dst). Tests: fresh-consumer scaffold+build, and idempotent re-init preserving a user edit. 59 tests pass, ruff clean.

title: Add install provenance/state tracking (.basicly/state/install.json)
description: §9/§11.8: record installed catalog version + provenance so 'update' can reason about upgrades and 'check' can report drift. [Planned].

title: Implement 'basicly hooks-build' (project core/hooks into consumer pre-commit wiring)
description: §11.6: project core/hooks/*.py + generate/merge .pre-commit-config.yaml entries (and/or .git/hooks) into a consumer repo. Write only changed bytes, update manifest, matching 'build' semantics. Must not clobber unrelated pre-commit hooks the consumer already has.
close_reason: Implemented 'basicly hooks-build': added tool-agnostic hooks.yaml manifest + src/basicly/hooks.py that materializes hook scripts (write-if-changed) and merges a managed repo:local block into .pre-commit-config.yaml, preserving foreign repos/hooks and idempotent on re-run. Verified e2e via uvx: init -> hooks-build produces a config pointing at .basicly/core/hooks/*.py. init next-steps updated to include hooks-build.

title: Implement 'basicly hooks-check' (staleness parity for projected hooks)
description: §11.6: byte-for-byte staleness check of projected hooks + wiring vs the catalog, exit 1 on mismatch, no auto-fix — same contract as 'check' / 'skills-check'.
close_reason: Implemented 'basicly hooks-check': byte-for-byte staleness of materialized scripts vs bundled catalog + managed pre-commit wiring; exit 1 with reasons on mismatch, no auto-fix (mirrors check/skills-check). Tests cover foreign-hook preservation, idempotency, round-trip pass, and wiring-drift detection. Verified e2e via uvx (passes after hooks-build).

title: Implement override validation (replaces exists / override:true required / no mutual user-user replaces)
description: §5/§11.3: planner._apply_user_replacements removes core fragments listed in 'replaces' but never validates them. Enforce: replaces targets must exist; override:true required before a replacement is honored; reject two user fragments that replace each other. Hard errors, with tests.
close_reason: Implemented replaces/override validation in loader._validate_replacements with tests

title: Add 'enforced_by' schema field + enforcement-pointer lint (§3.1)
description: §3.1/§11.4: context minimalism has no mechanical check. Add an 'enforced_by' field to the fragment schema and a lint that flags prose restating a mechanically-enforced rule instead of pointing at the command/config.

title: Implement 'basicly verify' + 'basicly build --verify'
description: §6/§11.5: standalone deterministic gate beyond schema/duplicate-id (duplicate/near-duplicate bodies, static-dictionary contradiction, vague-phrase ambiguity, scope-overlap), plus override validation and the enforced_by check. 'build --verify' runs verify first and writes nothing on failure. Depends on D1 + D2.

title: Implement 'basicly review' (agent-assisted semantic review, advisory)
description: §6/§11.5: advisory semantic pass where an agent reads the rendered diff for contradiction/ambiguity. Report, not a merge gate. Post-MVP; depends on the deterministic 'verify' existing first.

title: Design + implement Python skill source schema and loader change
description: §11.7: define the structured Python (non-SKILL.md) source format, update schema + skills loader to read it. Migrate existing core/skills/*/SKILL.md sources. Half of a schema+loader+projector change.

title: Implement skill projector: emit SKILL.md from the new source format
description: §11.7: update skills-build/skills-check to project the new Python source into SKILL.md at target roots only, so the discoverable copy exists exactly once. Depends on E1.

title: curl bootstrap shim for consumers without uv/Python
description: §9/§11.8: thin curl|sh shim over the same basicly commands for consumers lacking uv/Python. [Planned].

title: Catalog selection / flavors (install fragments+hooks but not skills)
description: §9/§11.8: let a consumer choose a subset of the catalog at install time. [Planned].

title: Codex scoped rules (.codex/rules/*.rules) renderer
description: §4.4/§11.8: Codex currently gets only the shared AGENTS.md baseline; scoped rules are [Deferred].

title: Cursor target renderer + templates
description: §11.9: Cursor as a target is [Deferred] — no renderer, no templates yet.

title: Packaging & distribution: make basicly uvx-installable
description: §9 / §11.1-11.2: packaging is disabled (no [build-system] table, tool.uv.package=false, catalog lives outside src/). Until fixed 'uvx --from git+... basicly' cannot resolve basicly.cli. Makes the package buildable/installable and the catalog shippable.
close_reason: All four children closed: jinja2 runtime dep (8if), hatchling packaging + entry point (8a7), catalog bundling (juj), end-to-end uvx verification (8u2). basicly is now uvx-installable with the catalog shipped in the distribution.

title: basicly init: scaffold & materialize catalog in a consumer repo
description: §8/§11.8: 'basicly init' is [Planned]. A real consumer needs an explicit command that lays down basicly.toml, the overlay skeleton, and the managed core catalog materialized from the installed package.

title: Ship the gates half: hooks-build / hooks-check projection
description: §11.6: core/hooks/*.py are a dogfooded catalog location but a fresh consumer has no command to install them into .git/hooks / .pre-commit-config.yaml. Without this the deterministic 'gates' half (§0) can't be installed. Mirrors the build/check contract.
close_reason: Both children closed: hooks-build (lku) and hooks-check (t51). Gates half is now installable in a consumer repo.

title: Catalog integrity: override validation & verification pipeline
description: §5/§6/§11.3-11.5: extension mechanism and deterministic verification are only partly built. Harden correctness so a consumer's overrides and catalog can't silently break.

title: Migrate skill source format from SKILL.md to a non-discoverable format
description: §4.2/§11.7: catalog skills authored as SKILL.md risk double-loading. Decided fix: author skills in a non-SKILL.md format and project to SKILL.md only at target roots.

title: Post-release distribution backlog (deferred in §11.8-11.9)
description: Captured so nothing is lost; explicitly out of scope for v0.1 per architecture §11.8-11.9.

title: hooks projection rewrites/compares whole pre-commit config instead of its managed block
description: Review findings 1+2: check_hooks compares full file text against a yaml.safe_dump re-render, so any hand-formatted config (including this repo's own dogfooded one — verified, exits 1) is permanently 'stale'; hooks-build round-trips the whole file through safe_load/safe_dump, destroying comments and reformatting foreign blocks, contradicting the 'never clobbered' contract. Fix: compare managed hooks semantically (parse + subset match by id); rewrite the file only when a managed hook is actually missing/wrong; warn when a rewrite normalizes formatting. Also validate hooks.yaml entries with a clear error instead of a bare KeyError.
close_reason: hooks projection now compares/edits only its managed hooks semantically: managed_hook_mismatches() parses the config and subset-matches each managed hook by id (formatting/comments/block-grouping agnostic); sync_hooks rewrites the co-owned file only when a managed hook is missing/wrong, with a CLI note when a rewrite normalizes formatting; invalid YAML raises a clear error; hooks.yaml entries validated (no bare KeyError). Regression tests: dogfood config passes its own gate (was exit 1), comments survive no-op sync, tampered entry detected+repaired. 67 tests pass.

title: init hardcodes .basicly/core and default-config literal duplicates config.py defaults
description: Review findings 3+4+10: cmd_init materializes into a hardcoded .basicly/core while build/hooks-build honor basicly.toml — with a custom core path, init->build silently generates nothing (reproduced). _core_hooks_dir also derives hooks from core_fragments.parent inconsistently with init. Fix: single core_root notion on ProjectPaths used by init and hooks; guard against absolute core paths leaking into committed config; move the scaffolded basicly.toml default into config.py next to the path defaults with a test asserting they agree.
close_reason: cmd_init now materializes into ProjectPaths.core_root (derived once from core_fragments.parent) instead of a hardcoded .basicly/core; _core_hooks_dir uses the same notion and rejects absolute paths (would bake machine-specific paths into the shared pre-commit config). The scaffolded basicly.toml moved to config.DEFAULT_CONFIG_TOML next to the built-in defaults with a test asserting parse-equality. Regression test reproduces the custom-path consumer flow: init -> build -> hooks-build all target the configured root. 70 tests pass.

title: pre-commit entry string is built unquoted — paths with spaces break every commit
description: Review finding 5: _hook_entry f-string concatenates the script path into 'entry' unquoted; pre-commit shlex-splits entry, so a core path containing a space yields a hook that fails on every commit. Fix: shlex.quote the script path.
close_reason: shlex.quote the script path inside the pre-commit entry string; test covers space-containing path round-trip and that plain paths stay unquoted (dogfood config semantics unchanged — live hooks-check still passes).

title: bundled_catalog_root: stale packaged dir shadows authoring source; parents[2] depth assumption fragile
description: Review finding 7: bare is_dir() preference means a stale src/basicly/catalog dir (or stale site-packages snapshot) silently shadows the live .basicly/core; parents[2] breaks if catalog.py becomes a subpackage. Fix: walk parents for the source-checkout marker (src/basicly/catalog.py + .basicly/core) first, fall back to the packaged copy; also dedup the catalog file-walk (cli._materialize_catalog vs hooks._iter_catalog_files) into catalog.py. Make test_catalog fallback test assert deterministically.
close_reason: bundled_catalog_root now walks parents for the source-tree marker (src/basicly/catalog.py + .basicly/core) FIRST, so the live authoring source always wins over a stale projected copy (leftover src/basicly/catalog dir or stale in-repo venv site-packages snapshot); no more fixed parents[2] depth assumption. Installed wheels (no such ancestor) resolve the packaged copy — verified live via uvx from outside any source tree. Walker deduped: iter_catalog_files in catalog.py is now the single definition used by init materialization and hooks projection. Catalog fallback test made deterministic (no silent assertion-free pass). 71 tests pass.

title: architecture §9 contradicts §8 on init status and states unverified git+ install as fact
description: Review finding 8: §9 summary still lists 'basicly init' as a remaining gap/[Planned] while §8 marks it [Implemented] in the same diff; §9 also claims the git+ install path resolves although only the local-wheel path was exercised (commits unpushed). Fix both statements.
close_reason: §9 no longer lists init as a remaining gap/[Planned] (now [Implemented], consistent with §8), and the git+<remote>@<ref> install claim is stated honestly: verified from a local wheel, structurally validated for git+ via the sdist, unverified against a pushed ref.

title: unify projection engines: shared SyncResult, write-if-changed, and check/build report helpers across skills/hooks/init
description: Review finding 9 (remainder after walker dedup in the locator bug): HookSyncResult duplicates SkillSyncResult, three _write_if_changed variants exist (cli, hooks, skills), and cmd_hooks_build/check are copies of cmd_skills_build/check. Extract one SyncResult + write-if-changed + _report_sync/_report_mismatches used by all three engines; align comparison semantics (bytes vs text/CRLF).

title: quality-gate fragment: add dogfood-gate and verification-scope rules (session retro)
description: Retro from the v0.1 release session: (1) hooks-check shipped failing in the dogfood repo because verification covered only the greenfield consumer flow; (2) architecture §9 stated the unverified git+ install as fact. Add two quality-gate fragment rules: run new/changed check commands against this repo itself, and state docs verification scope honestly. Approved by user.
close_reason: Added the two approved rules to quality-gate.fragment.md (tightened wording), regenerated AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/copilot-instructions.md via basicly build; dogfood gates green. Copilot size-cap overrun tracked separately.

title: tool-br skill: warn against hand-rolled bulk-create shell loops
description: Retro: ${var:+--flag} conditional-flag expansion in a bulk-create shell loop word-split and silently broke --parent linkage (6 failed creates, 18 orphaned tasks needing repair). Add a Common Pitfalls bullet pointing at 'br create --file <markdown>' bulk import or explicit per-issue calls, and verifying hierarchy with br epic status / br dep tree. Approved by user.
close_reason: Added the bulk-create pitfall bullet to the catalog tool-br SKILL.md and reprojected to .claude/skills via skills-build (default root only — --all-default-roots sprawl reverted).

title: copilot-instructions.md exceeds 6000-char cap — split per §7, don't shrink
description: The copilot target was already at 5999/6000 chars; any fragment addition now overflows it (currently 6271 after the retro quality-gate lines). Per architecture §7 the remedy is splitting content into path-scoped .github/instructions/*.instructions.md rules (or trimming restated-enforcement prose per §3.1), not shrinking judgment prose. Needs a content pass over the copilot projection.

title: Improve agent-config catalog and fix scoped-fragment projection
close_reason: improve fragment catalog, fix scoped-fragment projection, unify size cap, rewrite architecture diagram and docs

title: Add distributable git identity guard hook, setup script, and skill guidance
close_reason: add identity-guard pre-commit hook, generic setup_git_identity script, tool-git skill guidance, and tests

title: Pin beads issue prefix in tracked config and ignore transient br artifacts
close_reason: pin prefix in tracked config, ignore transient br artifacts, document no-slug id rule

title: Activate and harden distributed git-hook gates
description: Root cause of the identity/commit-message failures: basicly ships gate scripts but never activates them. .git/hooks had only samples, so identity-guard, commit-msg, beads-commit-msg, and pre-push never ran locally; CI was the only enforcement. Umbrella for: validator dotted-id fix, identity-guard into the manifest, hooks-build install + hooks-check verify, and a shippable worktree isolation script.

title: Accept dotted hierarchical beads ids in commit-msg validator
description: commit-msg.py HEADER_PATTERN issue-id group allows only [a-z0-9] and hyphen, so dotted child ids like basicly-zrj.8 are rejected and absorbed into the description, failing validation. beads-commit-msg.py already accepts them. Align commit-msg.py to accept dotted ids; add tests.
close_reason: commit-msg.py accepts dotted hierarchical beads ids; conventional-commits skill genericized and documents the dotted ids plus local-vs-CI parity

title: Promote identity-guard into hooks.yaml manifest
description: identity-guard.py is hand-wired only in this repo .pre-commit-config.yaml and absent from core/hooks/hooks.yaml, so hooks-build never projects it to consumers and hooks-check never verifies it. Add it to the manifest as a pre-commit stage always_run hook; update tests and regenerate wiring.
close_reason: identity-guard added to core/hooks/hooks.yaml so hooks-build projects it to consumers and hooks-check verifies it; tests cover the manifest entry

title: hooks-build installs git hooks and hooks-check verifies installation
description: hooks-build only writes .pre-commit-config.yaml and prints a manual pre-commit install hint, so gates stay inert until someone runs install. Make hooks-build run pre-commit install for all stages (guarded) and hooks-check report when hooks are not installed. Prevents shipped-but-inactive gates.
close_reason: hooks-build now runs pre-commit install for every managed stage after wiring (--no-install opts out; degrades gracefully without pre-commit); hooks-check warns non-fatally when git hooks are not installed; tests + docs updated

title: Rewrite invalid historical commit messages on main
description: The unpushed batch merged to main contains commit messages violating the conventional-commit validator (slashes, semicolons, section sign, plus, quotes, dotted prose). Reword descriptions to conform and force-push main, keeping authors correct. Depends on the dotted-id validator fix.
close_reason: reworded the 9 invalid historical commit subjects to the house style (dropped slashes/semicolons/section-sign/plus/quotes/dotted-prose); dotted-id-only commits pass via the ed2.1 validator fix; main force-pushed

title: Shippable generic worktree isolation script plus skill
description: Provide a genericized, catalog-shipped worktree helper (create sibling <repo>.worktrees/<name> on harness/<name>, provision deps, verify identity, install hooks, safe cleanup) plus a skill that points at it, so consumers get correct worktree isolation on install. Sibling placement chosen over in-repo .claude/worktrees per agent-harness/dreidesk convention.
close_reason: Superseded by basicly-onb.1 (worktree isolation feature) under the harness epic basicly-onb, which carries the decomposed tasks and acceptance criteria.

title: Add committed trusted-workstation Claude permissions (settings.json)
description: Add a tracked .claude/settings.json with the trusted-workstation model: blanket-allow safe tools (Bash/Edit/Write/Read/Glob/Grep/web) plus a deny floor (rm -rf, force-push, --no-verify/--no-gpg-sign, reset --hard, clean -f, filter-branch/repo, .env reads). Makes the no-prompt-spam baseline portable to fresh clones instead of relying on machine-local settings.
close_reason: Added committed .claude/settings.json with trusted-workstation model (blanket allow + deny floor)

title: Document harness architecture and fill tool-br skill gaps for the harness base
description: Base for the harness work: (1) add architecture.md Part 12 specifying the agent-agnostic development loop (work classes = br types, fractal tracks, checkpoints, parallel-build/serial-merge, deterministic-block/AI-advisory gates, br as state substrate, sibling worktrees, resumability, ship, positioning vs agent-harness). (2) fill tool-br skill gaps: gate/scheduler/lint, acceptance-criteria/design/notes/external-ref/agent-context fields, dep tree/cycles, comments, coordination, sync --merge; correct the status enum (no rework status). Confirms bv is not a harness runtime dependency.
close_reason: architecture.md Part 12 (harness spec) added; tool-br skill filled with gate/scheduler/lint/fields/deps/comments/coordination/sync--merge and corrected status enum

title: basicly harness: agent-agnostic development loop
description: Thin-over-br engine that binds work isolation + workflow loop + hard verify/validate gates into one predictable, cross-agent, resumable machine (architecture.md Part 12).
acceptance_criteria: The full loop (intake->classify->decompose->build->verify->validate->ship->retro) runs end-to-end on a real feature in this repo, agent-agnostic, resumable via br, with deterministic gates blocking, worktrees isolating and cleaning up, and no manual JSONL conflict editing.

title: Work isolation: sibling git worktrees lifecycle + skill
description: basicly worktree create/cleanup: sibling <repo>.worktrees/<name> on harness/<name>, provision uv+npm deps, install pre-commit hooks, safe teardown. Reference agent-harness worktree_session.py know-how (copy-mode deps, merge-tree probe, mode-aware cleanup). Supersedes basicly-ed2.5.
acceptance_criteria: basicly worktree create makes a sibling <repo>.worktrees/<name> on harness/<name>, provisions uv+npm deps and installs pre-commit hooks; cleanup removes the worktree and merged branch; trivial work bypasses isolation; cross-platform; dogfooded in this repo.
close_reason: All six children closed. basicly worktree create/cleanup/list + bg-isolation implemented: sibling <repo>.worktrees/<name> on harness/<name>, uv+npm provisioning, pre-commit hooks installed, safe mode-aware cleanup with merged-branch deletion (main untouched), concurrency cap + base branch configurable, agent-agnostic worktree-isolation skill projected to 3 targets, consent-gated Claude bgIsolation setting. Dogfooded end to end in this repo; 112 tests pass.

title: Verify runner: config-driven CI checks reporting to br gate
description: Adapt beads-blueprint validate.py structure (fast/full/staged modes) but config-driven per consumer (not hardcoded Python); run the repo's configured checks; record a required gate via br gate report.
acceptance_criteria: A config-driven verify runner runs the consumer's configured checks (not hardcoded), returns pass/fail with fast/full/staged modes, and records a required gate via br gate report; green on this repo.
close_reason: Config-driven verify runner: [verify].checks in basicly.toml + load_verify_config; src/basicly/verify.py runs checks for fast/full/staged (staged filters to staged files), aggregates pass/fail/skip, records a gate via br gate report. CLI 'basicly verify [--mode|--issue|--gate]', nonzero exit on fail. 11 unit tests + real dogfood green on this repo across all 3 modes; gate recorded on this issue. 123 tests pass.

comments.author: visicni
comments.text: NEXT-SESSION PLAN (verify runner). Prior art: /home/visicni/Development/beads-blueprint/validate.py (239 lines; hardcoded checks ruff/ruff-format/pyright/bandit/pip-audit/markdownlint/pytest; modes: default, --commit=staged, --fast, --full). Make it CONFIG-DRIVEN, not hardcoded. br interface (verified): 'br gate report --gate <name> --provider <provider> --status pass|fail <issue-id>'; required-gate status via 'br gate list'. Approach: (1) [verify] section in basicly.toml declaring checks per mode + load_verify_config in config.py, mirroring the load_worktree_config pattern from onb.1.3; (2) new src/basicly/verify.py runner: run configured checks for a mode, collect pass/fail, staged-file awareness for 'staged'; (3) record a REQUIRED gate via br gate report, nonzero exit on failure so the loop can block; (4) CLI 'basicly verify [--mode fast|full|staged] [--issue <id>]' + tests + dogfood GREEN on this repo (ruff/pyright/pytest already configured). Critical path: onb.2 is the only unblocker of onb.3; onb.2+3+4+5 all gate the onb.6 loop engine.

title: Gate and checkpoint policy engine
description: DoR via br lint before decomposition passes; deterministic gate fail blocks advance; AI-semantic advisory never blocks; n=2 rework then escalate; 3 human checkpoints (classify/decompose/ship). Policy in the engine; verdicts in br gate.
acceptance_criteria: DoR enforced via br lint before the decomposition checkpoint passes; a failed deterministic gate blocks advance; AI-semantic results are advisory and never block; rework retries at most twice then escalates to a human; the three human checkpoints (classify/decompose/ship) are enforced.
close_reason: Gate/checkpoint policy engine: [policy] config (required_gates, max_rework) + src/basicly/policy.py. DoR via br lint; gate_status via br gate list (failed/missing required gate blocks, other gates advisory never block); bounded rework loop + 3 human checkpoints recorded as inspectable br comment markers (no side-state, since gate results overwrite). CLI 'basicly policy {dor,gate,checkpoint,rework}'. 13 unit tests (fake br) + live dogfood (gate ALLOWED/BLOCKED, DoR, checkpoint approve, rework n=2 escalate). 134 tests pass.

comments.author: visicni
comments.text: NEXT-SESSION PLAN (gate & checkpoint policy engine). Consumes the br gate verdicts wired in onb.2. VERIFIED br data shapes: 'br gate list <id> --robot' -> {results:[{gate,provider,passed(bool),note}], gated_transitions:[]}; 'br lint <id> --json' -> {results:[{missing:[...], suggestions:[...]}]} (DoR passes when missing==[]). Likely src/basicly/policy.py + thin CLI, called later by the loop engine (onb.6). Functions: definition_of_ready(repo_root,id) via br lint; required_gates_pass(repo_root,id) reading br gate list (deterministic fail blocks; AI-semantic/non-required advisory never blocks); rework_state(id) enforcing n=2-then-escalate; checkpoint helpers for the 3 human checkpoints (classify/decompose/ship). RESOLVE FIRST (br is the only state store, no side-state): (1) how 'required' gates are represented -- gated_transitions vs a [policy]/[gate] config listing required gate names; (2) where the rework counter lives -- derived by counting passed:false gate results, or a br comment/--external-ref; (3) how the 3 checkpoint approvals are recorded -- a gate, a comment, or a status marker. onb.3/4/5 are mutually independent; all three gate onb.6.

title: Decomposer and dependency-graph builder
description: Given a feature, produce child br issues + br dep graph; mark nodes parallel-safe only when file-disjoint scopes are predicted, else emit a fixed serial order; set acceptance criteria on children.
acceptance_criteria: Given a feature, the decomposer creates child br issues with a br dep graph and per-child acceptance criteria; nodes are marked parallel-safe only when file-disjoint scopes are predicted, otherwise a fixed serial order is emitted.

title: Merge orchestrator: serial merge queue + conflict-resolver
description: Parallel-build, serial-merge: land worktrees one at a time in topological order, re-verify after each; conflict-resolver (agent+scripts+skills) under n=2 to human; reconcile .beads/issues.jsonl with br sync --merge (br has no git merge-driver).
acceptance_criteria: Parallel worktrees land through a serial merge queue in topological order, re-verifying after each merge; the conflict-resolver handles residual conflicts under n=2->human; .beads/issues.jsonl is reconciled with br sync --merge; no git conflict markers are hand-edited.

title: Loop engine: intake, classify, fractal tracks, resumable state machine
description: Intake any input -> classify to a br type -> nested tracks (bug/task/feature/epic) -> state machine with the 3 checkpoints; state lives in br (external-ref for worktree/branch, agent-context inherited down the tree); resumable across restart and across agents.
acceptance_criteria: Intake classifies to a br type and runs the nested tracks; the state machine enforces the checkpoints; all durable state is in br (external-ref + agent-context); the loop resumes correctly after a restart and when switching agents (claude->codex/copilot).

title: Agent-agnostic runner adapters (claude, codex, copilot)
description: One runner interface drives claude/codex/copilot headless (command template, headless flags, prompt injection, output capture); selected by capability detection or explicit flag; dry-run prints the exact per-runner command. Shared seam with agent-harness.
acceptance_criteria: One runner interface drives claude, codex, and copilot headless; the runner is chosen by capability detection or an explicit flag; a dry-run prints the exact command each runner would execute; the loop behaves identically regardless of runner.

title: Projected orchestration guidance (agent-agnostic skills)
description: Author the loop as agent-agnostic fragments/skills that project to Claude/Codex/Copilot; adapt beads-blueprint orchestration prose; passes basicly build + skills-check.
acceptance_criteria: The loop is authored as agent-agnostic fragments/skills that project to Claude/Codex/Copilot (adapted from beads-blueprint orchestration prose) and passes basicly build and skills-check.

title: Worktree create + provision
description: Create sibling <repo>.worktrees/<name> on harness/<name> off the recorded base branch; provision deps (uv sync + npm install) and install pre-commit hooks for all stages.
acceptance_criteria: Running create yields a worktree at ../<repo>.worktrees/<name> on harness/<name> with a working .venv, node_modules, and installed pre-commit/commit-msg/pre-push hooks.
close_reason: Implemented worktree.create(): sibling worktree on harness branch, uv sync + npm install provisioning, git hook install, session record in git common dir. Unit tests + real dogfood create/verify passed.

title: Worktree cleanup + teardown
description: git worktree remove + delete the merged branch after a node lands; detect stale/abandoned worktrees (br coordination); safe because deps are copied, not linked.
acceptance_criteria: Cleanup removes the worktree dir and deletes the merged branch; main is never touched; a stale worktree is detectable and reclaimable.
close_reason: Added worktree.cleanup() (remove dir + prune + delete harness branch + drop record, base untouched, git-list fallback, keep record on unmerged branch) and stale_sessions() detection. Unit tests + real create/cleanup dogfood passed.

title: basicly worktree CLI subcommands + config
description: Wire create/cleanup as basicly worktree subcommands; config for base branch and concurrency cap (default 4); cross-platform paths.
acceptance_criteria: basicly worktree create/cleanup work cross-platform; concurrency cap and base branch are configurable (cap default 4).
close_reason: Wired basicly worktree create/cleanup/list into the CLI with [worktree] config (base_branch + concurrency cap default 4) via load_worktree_config. create honors configured base and enforces the cap; list marks stale. Unit tests + real CLI create/list/cleanup dogfood passed.

title: worktree-isolation skill (agent-agnostic, projected)
description: Author the sibling-worktree convention as an agent-agnostic skill projected to Claude/Codex/Copilot; documents create/provision/install-hooks/cleanup and the never-in-repo rule.
acceptance_criteria: The skill projects to all three agent targets via basicly skills-build and states the sibling-placement + provision + install-hooks + cleanup rules.
close_reason: Authored .basicly/core/skills/worktree-isolation/SKILL.md (agent-agnostic): sibling placement on harness/<name>, never-in-repo rule, create/provision (uv sync+npm install)+hook install, cleanup, Claude bg-isolation note. Verified identical projection to claude/github/agents roots via skills-build; committed .claude projection; skills-check clean.

title: Worktree lifecycle tests + dogfood
description: Unit tests for path/branch/provision/cleanup logic; dogfood by creating and cleaning a real worktree of this repo end to end.
acceptance_criteria: Tests pass and a real worktree of this repo is created and cleaned end to end without touching main.
close_reason: Added provision_deps command-selection test (uv sync/npm install chosen by manifest, via real ref past autouse stub) and a create->cleanup test asserting base HEAD never moves. Real CLI dogfood: created worktree of this repo with working .venv+node_modules, cleaned up fully, main HEAD unchanged. 106 tests pass.

comments.author: visicni
comments.text: Mostly satisfied by the onb.1.1-1.3 session: 17 unit tests in tests/test_worktree.py cover path/branch/provision(stubbed)/cleanup, plus a real create->cleanup CLI dogfood of this repo (no main mutation). Remaining to close: add a test exercising provision_deps command-selection without a live install, and an explicit 'base/main untouched' assertion; then re-run + capture the dogfood.

title: Claude target install: set worktree bg-isolation to none for harness worktrees
description: Claude Code's background-isolation guard (worktree.bgIsolation, default on) forces a background agent to isolate into .claude/worktrees/ via EnterWorktree before editing, which conflicts with the harness's sibling <repo>.worktrees/ isolation (EnterWorktree cannot target a sibling path). To run the harness under Claude Code, bgIsolation must be none because the harness provides isolation itself. Settings precedence VERIFIED (code.claude.com/docs/en/settings): local settings.local.json (personal, gitignored) overrides project settings.json (team-shared, committed) overrides user global. So on install for the Claude target, basicly sets worktree.bgIsolation=none in the COMMITTED .claude/settings.json (team-wide default that ships with the repo); any user can override in their gitignored .claude/settings.local.json. Inform/explain the change and rationale and obtain consent before writing; never change it silently. Claude-target-only; Codex/Copilot have no such setting. May use the update-config mechanism.
acceptance_criteria: On install for the Claude target, basicly explains that worktree.bgIsolation will be set to none and why, obtains consent, then writes it to the committed .claude/settings.json (team-wide default), and documents that a user may override in the gitignored .claude/settings.local.json (verified precedence: local overrides project overrides user). Never changed silently; Codex/Copilot installs unaffected.
close_reason: Added claude_settings.py + 'basicly worktree bg-isolation' command: explains the bgIsolation conflict + rationale, writes worktree.bgIsolation=none to the COMMITTED .claude/settings.json only on explicit --yes (never silent), merge-preserves other keys, documents the gitignored settings.local.json override. Claude-only; Codex/Copilot untouched. 7 tests + real dogfood (explain-only no-write, then --yes applied to this repo's settings). 112 tests pass.

comments.author: visicni
comments.text: DESIGN GAP: no per-target install step exists yet. Renderers (src/basicly/renderers/*.py) only emit text; cmd_init scaffolds; nothing writes .claude/settings.json. This task must FIRST add an install mechanism (extend init, a new 'basicly install --target claude', or a settings step) before it can consent-write worktree.bgIsolation=none to the committed .claude/settings.json. Config-file write => explicit user consent required (bead design + CLAUDE.md rule).
